docs.title: agent-a11y
docs.text: Go accessibility auditing toolkit for WCAG 2.0, 2.1, and 2.2 compliance testing.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
agent-a11y provides comprehensive accessibility testing capabilities:

  • WCAG Compliance - Test against WCAG 2.0, 2.1, and 2.2 at levels A, AA, and AAA
  • Multiple Audit Modes - Single page, site crawling, and user journey testing
  • axe-core Integration - Industry-standard accessibility rules
  • LLM-as-a-Judge - AI evaluation to reduce false positives
  • Multiple Output Formats - JSON, HTML, Markdown, VPAT 2.4, WCAG-EM, CSV
  • Flexible Integration - CLI, HTTP API, MCP server, Go library

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
CLIGo Library 
# Audit a page
agent-a11y audit https://example.com --level AA --version 2.2

# Output HTML report
agent-a11y audit https://example.com -o report.html --format html

auditor, _ := a11y.New(
    a11y.WithLevel(a11y.LevelAA),
    a11y.WithVersion(a11y.Version22),
)
defer auditor.Close()

result, _ := auditor.AuditPage(ctx, "https://example.com")
fmt.Println(result.Summary())

docs.title: Audit Types
docs.text: Type Description Use Case Single Page Audit one URL Quick checks, CI pipelines Site Crawl Crawl and audit multiple pages Full site audits Journey Follow user journeys Dynamic content, SPAs

docs.title: WCAG Coverage
docs.text:
• WCAG 2.0, 2.1, 2.2
  • Levels A, AA, AAA
  • Specialized tests for keyboard, focus, reflow, and more

docs.title: Report Formats
docs.text:
• JSON - Machine-readable for CI/CD integration
  • HTML - Interactive reports for stakeholders
  • VPAT 2.4 - Voluntary Product Accessibility Template
  • WCAG-EM - W3C evaluation methodology format
  • CSV - Spreadsheet export

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
1. Install agent-a11y
  2. Run your first audit
  3. Configure for your needs

docs.title: Integration Options
docs.text:
• CLI - Command-line tool for scripts and CI/CD
  • HTTP API - REST API for web services
  • MCP Server - Integration with AI assistants (Claude, etc.)
  • Go Library - Embed in your Go applications
  • Multi-Agent Spec - Use in multi-agent workflows

docs.title: a11y (root)
docs.text:
Public API and result types.

  • Auditor - Main entry point
  • Result - Audit results
  • Finding - Individual accessibility issue

docs.title: audit
docs.text:
Core audit engine.

  • Engine - Orchestrates audit process
  • AuditResult - Internal result type
  • Finding - Internal finding type

docs.title: audit/axe
docs.text:
axe-core integration.

  • Injects axe-core into pages
  • Parses axe-core results
  • Maps to WCAG criteria

docs.title: audit/specialized
docs.text:
Browser-based specialized tests.

  • Keyboard navigation
  • Focus management
  • Reflow testing
  • Target size
  • Text spacing

docs.title: audit/rules
docs.text:
WCAG rule definitions.

  • Rule registry
  • Criteria mapping
  • Impact classification

docs.title: crawler
docs.text:
Website crawler.

  • Link extraction
  • SPA detection
  • Depth/limit management
  • Robots.txt respect

docs.title: journey
docs.text:
Journey-based testing.

  • YAML journey compiler
  • Step executor
  • State management

docs.title: llm
docs.text:
LLM-as-a-Judge integration.

  • Provider abstraction
  • Prompt templates
  • Result parsing

docs.title: report
docs.text:
Report generation.

  • JSON, HTML, Markdown

  • WCAG-EM
  • CSV

docs.title: remediation
docs.text:
Remediation task generation.

MCP server implementation.

docs.title: wcag

WCAG criteria database.

docs.title: types
docs.text:
Shared types.

  • Impact levels

docs.title: config
docs.text:
Configuration management.

  • File loading

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
1. Input - URL from CLI, API, MCP, or library
  2. Crawling (optional) - Discover pages via crawler
  3. Browser - Load page via vibium-go
  4. axe-core - Run accessibility rules
  5. Specialized - Run browser-based tests
  6. LLM (optional) - Evaluate findings
  7. Aggregation - Combine results
  8. Reporting - Generate output format
  9. Output - Return to caller

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• vibium-go - Browser automation
  • omnillm - LLM provider abstraction
  • multi-agent-spec - Multi-agent integration
  • cobra - CLI framework
  • MCP SDK - Model Context Protocol

docs.title: Go Install
docs.text:
The easiest way to install agent-a11y:

go install github.com/plexusone/agent-a11y/cmd/agent-a11y@latest

docs.title: From Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/plexusone/agent-a11y.git
cd agent-a11y
go build -o agent-a11y ./cmd/agent-a11y

docs.title: Go Version
docs.text: agent-a11y requires Go 1.25 or later.

docs.title: Browser
docs.text:
For browser-based testing, you need Chrome or Chromium installed. agent-a11y uses the Vibium browser automation library which manages browser instances.

The browser is discovered in this order:

  1. VIBIUM_CLICKER_PATH environment variable
  2. System PATH
  3. Default installation locations

docs.title: LLM API Keys (Optional)
docs.text:
For LLM-enhanced evaluation, set the appropriate API key:

# Anthropic
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="your-key"

# OpenAI
export OPENAI_API_KEY="your-key"

# Google
export GOOGLE_API_KEY="your-key"

# xAI
export XAI_API_KEY="your-key"

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
agent-a11y version

Expected output:

agent-a11y v0.1.0 (commit: abc1234, built: 2026-02-28)

docs.title: Go Library
docs.text:
To use agent-a11y as a Go library:

go get github.com/plexusone/agent-a11y@latest

Then import:

import a11y "github.com/plexusone/agent-a11y"

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide walks you through running your first accessibility audit.

docs.title: Basic Audit
docs.text:
Run an audit on any URL:

agent-a11y audit https://example.com

Output:

WCAG 2.2 Level AA: Non-Conformant (Score: 75/100)
Issues: 1 critical, 2 serious, 5 moderate, 3 minor

Critical Issues:
  - [img-alt] Images must have alternate text

Serious Issues:
  - [label] Form elements must have labels
  - [color-contrast] Elements must have sufficient color contrast

docs.title: Specify WCAG Level and Version
docs.text:
# Test against WCAG 2.1 Level AAA
agent-a11y audit https://example.com --level AAA --version 2.1

docs.title: HTML Report
docs.text: agent-a11y audit https://example.com -o report.html --format html

docs.title: JSON Report
docs.text: agent-a11y audit https://example.com -o report.json --format json

docs.title: VPAT Report
docs.text: agent-a11y audit https://example.com -o vpat.html --format vpat

docs.title: Site Crawling
docs.text:
Audit an entire website:

agent-a11y audit https://example.com --crawl --depth 2 --max-pages 50

Options:

  • --crawl - Enable site crawling
  • --depth - Maximum crawl depth (default: 2)
  • --max-pages - Maximum pages to audit (default: 50)
  • --delay - Delay between requests (default: 500ms)

docs.title: LLM-Enhanced Evaluation
docs.text:
Reduce false positives with AI evaluation:

export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="your-key"

agent-a11y audit https://example.com \
  --llm-provider anthropic \
  --llm-model claude-sonnet-4-20250514

The LLM evaluates each finding and provides:

  • Confirmation or rejection of the issue
  • Reasoning for the decision
  • Confidence score

docs.title: Using as a Go Library
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "context"
    "fmt"
    "log"

    a11y "github.com/plexusone/agent-a11y"
)

func main() {
    ctx := context.Background()

    // Create auditor with options
    auditor, err := a11y.New(
        a11y.WithLevel(a11y.LevelAA),
        a11y.WithVersion(a11y.Version22),
        a11y.WithHeadless(true),
    )
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }
    defer auditor.Close()

    // Audit a page
    result, err := auditor.AuditPage(ctx, "https://example.com")
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    // Print summary
    fmt.Println(result.Summary())

    // Check conformance
    if result.Conformant() {
        fmt.Println("Site is conformant!")
    } else {
        fmt.Printf("Found %d critical issues\n", result.Stats.Critical)
    }

    // Generate HTML report
    html, _ := result.HTML()
    os.WriteFile("report.html", html, 0600)
}

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Learn about different audit types
  • Configure agent-a11y
  • Set up LLM integration

docs.title: Audit Types
docs.text: agent-a11y supports three types of accessibility audits.

docs.title: Single Page Audit
docs.text:
The simplest audit type - test a single URL.

agent-a11y audit https://example.com

Use cases:

  • Quick accessibility checks
  • CI/CD pipeline validation
  • Testing specific pages

Go library:

result, err := auditor.AuditPage(ctx, "https://example.com")

docs.title: Site Crawl Audit
docs.text:
Crawl a website and audit multiple pages automatically.

agent-a11y audit https://example.com --crawl --depth 2 --max-pages 50

Options:

Flag Description Default --crawl Enable crawling false --depth Maximum link depth 2 --max-pages Maximum pages to audit 50 --delay Delay between requests 500ms

Use cases:

  • Full website accessibility audits
  • Pre-launch compliance checks
  • Ongoing monitoring

Go library:

result, err := auditor.AuditSite(ctx, "https://example.com",
    a11y.WithCrawlDepth(2),
    a11y.WithMaxPages(50),
)

docs.title: SPA Detection
docs.text:
The crawler automatically detects Single Page Applications and:

  • Waits for JavaScript hydration
  • Handles dynamic content loading
  • Follows client-side navigation

docs.title: Journey Audit
docs.text:
Test accessibility along specific user journeys defined in YAML.

agent-a11y audit https://example.com --journey journey.yaml

Journey file example:

name: Login Flow
steps:
  - name: Visit login page
    action: navigate
    url: /login
    audit: true

  - name: Enter credentials
    action: type
    selector: "#username"
    value: "testuser"

  - name: Submit form
    action: click
    selector: "#submit"
    waitFor: "#dashboard"
    audit: true

Use cases:

  • Testing authenticated pages
  • Multi-step form validation
  • Dynamic content that requires interaction
  • SPA navigation flows

Go library:

result, err := auditor.AuditJourney(ctx, "https://example.com", "journey.yaml")

docs.title: Specialized Tests
docs.text:
Beyond axe-core rules, agent-a11y includes specialized tests:

Test WCAG Criterion Description Keyboard 2.1.1, 2.1.2 Keyboard navigation and traps Focus 2.4.7, 2.4.11 Focus visibility and order Reflow 1.4.10 400% zoom reflow Target Size 2.5.8 Touch target dimensions Text Spacing 1.4.12 Text spacing override support Hover 1.4.13 Hover content accessibility Flash 2.3.1 Flashing content detection

Enable specialized tests:

agent-a11y audit https://example.com --specialized

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: agent-a11y can be configured via command-line flags, environment variables, or a configuration file.

docs.title: Configuration File
docs.text:
Create ~/.agent-a11y.yaml or specify with --config:

# WCAG settings
wcag:
  level: AA          # A, AA, or AAA
  version: "2.2"     # 2.0, 2.1, or 2.2

# Browser settings
browser:
  headless: true
  timeout: 30s

# LLM settings (optional)
llm:
  enabled: true
  provider: anthropic
  model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  # API key from environment: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

# Crawling settings
crawl:
  depth: 2
  maxPages: 50
  delay: 500ms

# Output settings
output:
  format: json       # json, html, markdown, vpat, wcag, csv
  verbose: false

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Anthropic API key OPENAI_API_KEY OpenAI API key GOOGLE_API_KEY Google AI API key XAI_API_KEY xAI API key VIBIUM_HEADLESS Run browser headless (0/1) VIBIUM_DEBUG Enable debug logging (0/1)

docs.title: Global Flags
docs.text: Flag Description --config Path to config file --verbose Enable verbose output --headless Run browser in headless mode --timeout Browser timeout

docs.title: Audit Flags
docs.text: Flag Description --level WCAG level (A, AA, AAA) --version WCAG version (2.0, 2.1, 2.2) --format Output format -o, --output Output file path --crawl Enable site crawling --depth Crawl depth --max-pages Maximum pages to crawl --delay Delay between requests --journey Journey definition file --specialized Run specialized tests

docs.title: LLM Flags
docs.text: Flag Description --llm-provider LLM provider (anthropic, openai, google, xai, ollama) --llm-model Model name --llm-api-key API key (or use environment variable)

docs.title: Go Library Configuration
docs.text:
auditor, err := a11y.New(
    // WCAG settings
    a11y.WithLevel(a11y.LevelAA),
    a11y.WithVersion(a11y.Version22),

    // Browser settings
    a11y.WithHeadless(true),
    a11y.WithTimeout(30 * time.Second),

    // LLM settings
    a11y.WithLLM("anthropic", "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", os.Getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")),

    // Logging
    a11y.WithLogger(slog.Default()),
)

docs.title: LLM Integration
docs.text: agent-a11y uses LLM-as-a-Judge to evaluate accessibility findings and reduce false positives.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. axe-core identifies potential accessibility issues
  2. For each finding, the LLM evaluates:
  3. Is this a true accessibility barrier?
  4. What is the severity?
  5. What is the recommended fix?
  6. Findings are filtered or annotated based on LLM evaluation

docs.title: Supported Providers
docs.text: Provider Models Environment Variable Anthropic claude-sonnet-4-20250514, claude-opus-4-20250514 ANTHROPIC_API_KEY OpenAI gpt-4o, gpt-4-turbo OPENAI_API_KEY Google gemini-2.0-flash GOOGLE_API_KEY xAI grok-2 XAI_API_KEY Ollama llama3, mistral, etc. (local)

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="your-key"

agent-a11y audit https://example.com \
  --llm-provider anthropic \
  --llm-model claude-sonnet-4-20250514

docs.title: Config File
docs.text:
llm:
  enabled: true
  provider: anthropic
  model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514

docs.title: Go Library
docs.text:
auditor, _ := a11y.New(
    a11y.WithLLM("anthropic", "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", apiKey),
)

docs.title: Evaluation Output
docs.text:
With LLM enabled, each finding includes:

{
  "ruleId": "color-contrast",
  "description": "Elements must have sufficient color contrast",
  "llmConfirmed": true,
  "llmReasoning": "The text has a contrast ratio of 3.2:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements of 4.5:1 for normal text.",
  "llmConfidence": 0.95
}

docs.title: Ollama (Local)
docs.text:
For local LLM evaluation without API keys:

# Start Ollama
ollama serve

# Pull a model
ollama pull llama3

# Use with agent-a11y
agent-a11y audit https://example.com \
  --llm-provider ollama \
  --llm-model llama3

docs.title: Cost Considerations
docs.text:
LLM evaluation adds API costs. To manage costs:

  • Use smaller models for initial scans
  • Enable LLM only for final audits
  • Set confidence thresholds to skip obvious issues
llm:
  enabled: true
  provider: anthropic
  model: claude-haiku-20250514  # Cheaper model
  confidenceThreshold: 0.8      # Skip low-confidence evaluations

docs.title: Report Formats
docs.text: agent-a11y supports multiple output formats for different use cases.

docs.title: JSON
docs.text:
Machine-readable format for CI/CD integration and programmatic processing.

agent-a11y audit https://example.com --format json -o report.json

Structure:

{
  "url": "https://example.com",
  "wcagVersion": "2.2",
  "wcagLevel": "AA",
  "score": 85,
  "conformant": false,
  "stats": {
    "totalPages": 1,
    "totalFindings": 5,
    "critical": 0,
    "serious": 2,
    "moderate": 2,
    "minor": 1
  },
  "pages": [...],
  "findings": [...]
}

docs.title: HTML
docs.text:
Interactive HTML report for stakeholders and manual review.

agent-a11y audit https://example.com --format html -o report.html

Features:

  • Sortable/filterable findings table
  • Expandable issue details
  • Screenshot annotations
  • Summary dashboard

docs.title: Markdown
docs.text:
Text-based format for documentation and version control.

agent-a11y audit https://example.com --format markdown -o report.md

docs.title: VPAT 2.4
docs.text:
Voluntary Product Accessibility Template for procurement and compliance documentation.

agent-a11y audit https://example.com --format vpat -o vpat.html

Includes:

  • Product information
  • WCAG 2.x conformance table
  • Success criteria evaluation
  • Remarks and explanations

docs.title: WCAG-EM
docs.text:
W3C Website Accessibility Conformance Evaluation Methodology format.

agent-a11y audit https://example.com --format wcag -o wcag-em.json

Follows:

  • WCAG-EM 1.0 specification
  • Evaluation scope definition
  • Sample selection documentation
  • Audit results

docs.title: CSV
docs.text:
Spreadsheet-compatible format for data analysis.

agent-a11y audit https://example.com --format csv -o findings.csv

Columns:

  • URL, Rule ID, Description, Impact, Level
  • Element, Selector, HTML snippet
  • Success Criteria, Help URL

docs.title: Go Library
docs.text:
result, _ := auditor.AuditPage(ctx, url)

// JSON
jsonBytes, _ := result.JSON()

// HTML
htmlBytes, _ := result.HTML()

// Markdown
mdBytes, _ := result.Markdown()

// VPAT
vpatBytes, _ := result.VPAT()

// WCAG-EM
wcagBytes, _ := result.WCAG()

docs.title: Starting the Server
docs.text: agent-a11y serve --port 8080

docs.title: POST /audit
docs.text:
Run an accessibility audit.

Request:

{
  "url": "https://example.com",
  "level": "AA",
  "version": "2.2",
  "crawl": {
    "enabled": false,
    "depth": 2,
    "maxPages": 50
  },
  "llm": {
    "enabled": true,
    "provider": "anthropic",
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
  }
}

Response:

{
  "url": "https://example.com",
  "wcagVersion": "2.2",
  "wcagLevel": "AA",
  "score": 85,
  "conformant": false,
  "stats": {
    "totalPages": 1,
    "totalFindings": 5,
    "critical": 0,
    "serious": 2,
    "moderate": 2,
    "minor": 1
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "id": "finding-1",
      "ruleId": "color-contrast",
      "description": "Elements must have sufficient color contrast",
      "impact": "serious",
      "level": "AA",
      "element": "p",
      "selector": "#content > p:nth-child(2)",
      "html": "<p style=\"color: #777\">...</p>",
      "successCriteria": ["1.4.3"],
      "help": "https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.x/color-contrast"
    }
  ]
}

Status Codes:

Code Description 200 Audit completed 400 Invalid request 500 Audit failed

docs.title: GET /health
docs.text:
Health check endpoint.

Response:

{
  "status": "healthy",
  "version": "0.1.0"
}

docs.title: cURL
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/audit \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "url": "https://example.com",
    "level": "AA",
    "version": "2.2"
  }'

docs.title: Python
docs.text:
import requests

response = requests.post("http://localhost:8080/audit", json={
    "url": "https://example.com",
    "level": "AA",
    "version": "2.2"
})

result = response.json()
print(f"Score: {result['score']}/100")

docs.title: JavaScript
docs.text:
const response = await fetch("http://localhost:8080/audit", {
  method: "POST",
  headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    url: "https://example.com",
    level: "AA",
    version: "2.2"
  })
});

const result = await response.json();
console.log(`Score: ${result.score}/100`);

docs.title: agent-a11y audit
docs.text:
Run an accessibility audit.

agent-a11y audit <url> [flags]

Arguments:

  • <url> - URL to audit (required)

Flags:

Flag Short Description Default --level WCAG level (A, AA, AAA) AA --version WCAG version (2.0, 2.1, 2.2) 2.2 --format -f Output format json --output -o Output file path stdout --crawl Enable site crawling false --depth Crawl depth 2 --max-pages Maximum pages 50 --delay Request delay 500ms --journey Journey file path --specialized Run specialized tests false --llm-provider LLM provider --llm-model LLM model --headless Headless browser true --timeout Browser timeout 30s

Examples:

# Basic audit
agent-a11y audit https://example.com

# WCAG 2.1 Level AAA
agent-a11y audit https://example.com --level AAA --version 2.1

# Site crawl with HTML report
agent-a11y audit https://example.com --crawl -o report.html -f html

# With LLM evaluation
agent-a11y audit https://example.com --llm-provider anthropic --llm-model claude-sonnet-4-20250514

docs.title: agent-a11y serve
docs.text:
Start the HTTP API server.

agent-a11y serve [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description Default --port Server port 8080 --host Server host localhost

docs.title: agent-a11y mcp serve
docs.text:
Start the MCP server for AI assistant integration.

agent-a11y mcp serve

Communicates via stdio for MCP protocol.

docs.title: agent-a11y compare
docs.text:
Compare accessibility between two URLs.

agent-a11y compare <before-url> <after-url> [flags]

Shows:

  • Issues fixed
  • Issues introduced
  • Issues unchanged

docs.title: agent-a11y demo
docs.text:
Run demo audits on sample sites.

# List available demos
agent-a11y demo list

# Run a specific demo
agent-a11y demo run w3c-bad

# Generate a demo site
agent-a11y demo generate my-demo

docs.title: agent-a11y version
docs.text:
Show version information.

agent-a11y version

docs.title: Global Flags
docs.text: Flag Description --config Config file path --verbose Verbose output --help Show help

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Description 0 Success (conformant) 1 Non-conformant (issues found) 2 Error (audit failed)

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go get github.com/plexusone/agent-a11y@latest

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
import a11y "github.com/plexusone/agent-a11y"

auditor, _ := a11y.New()
defer auditor.Close()

result, _ := auditor.AuditPage(ctx, "https://example.com")
fmt.Println(result.Summary())

docs.title: Creating an Auditor
docs.text:
auditor, err := a11y.New(
    a11y.WithLevel(a11y.LevelAA),
    a11y.WithVersion(a11y.Version22),
    a11y.WithHeadless(true),
    a11y.WithTimeout(30 * time.Second),
    a11y.WithLLM("anthropic", "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", apiKey),
    a11y.WithLogger(slog.Default()),
)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
defer auditor.Close()

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description WithLevel(level) WCAG level (LevelA, LevelAA, LevelAAA) WithVersion(version) WCAG version (Version20, Version21, Version22) WithHeadless(bool) Run browser headless WithTimeout(duration) Browser timeout WithLLM(provider, model, key) Enable LLM evaluation WithLogger(logger) Custom slog logger

docs.title: Audit Methods
docs.text:
// Single page
result, err := auditor.AuditPage(ctx, "https://example.com")

// Site crawl
result, err := auditor.AuditSite(ctx, "https://example.com",
    a11y.WithCrawlDepth(2),
    a11y.WithMaxPages(50),
    a11y.WithCrawlDelay(500 * time.Millisecond),
)

// Journey
result, err := auditor.AuditJourney(ctx, "https://example.com", "journey.yaml")

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
result.URL           // Audited URL
result.Score         // 0-100 score
result.Level         // Target level
result.Version       // WCAG version
result.Findings      // []Finding
result.Pages         // []PageResult (for site audits)
result.Stats         // Stats summary

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
// Check conformance
if result.Conformant() {
    // Passed
}

// Get summary
fmt.Println(result.Summary())

// Filter findings
criticalIssues := result.CriticalFindings()
seriousIssues := result.SeriousFindings()
levelAIssues := result.FindingsByLevel(a11y.LevelA)
contrastIssues := result.FindingsByCriterion("1.4.3")

// Generate reports
jsonBytes, _ := result.JSON()
htmlBytes, _ := result.HTML()
mdBytes, _ := result.Markdown()
vpatBytes, _ := result.VPAT()

docs.title: Multi-Agent Integration
docs.text:
// Convert to AgentResult for multi-agent workflows
agentResult := result.AgentResult()

// Get narrative section for reports
narrative := result.Narrative()

// Get team section for TeamReport
teamSection := result.TeamSection()

docs.title: Finding
docs.text:
type Finding struct {
    ID              string
    RuleID          string
    Description     string
    Help            string
    SuccessCriteria []string
    Level           string
    Impact          string
    Element         string
    Selector        string
    HTML            string
    PageURL         string
    LLMConfirmed    *bool
    LLMReasoning    string
}

docs.title: Stats
docs.text:
type Stats struct {
    TotalPages    int
    TotalFindings int
    Critical      int
    Serious       int
    Moderate      int
    Minor         int
    LevelA        int
    LevelAA       int
    LevelAAA      int
}

docs.title: Constants
docs.text:
// Levels
a11y.LevelA
a11y.LevelAA
a11y.LevelAAA

// Versions
a11y.Version20
a11y.Version21
a11y.Version22

docs.title: MCP Server Reference
docs.text: agent-a11y provides a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for integration with AI assistants.

docs.title: Starting the Server
docs.text:
agent-a11y mcp serve

The server communicates via stdio using the MCP protocol.

docs.title: Claude Desktop Integration
docs.text:
Add to your Claude Desktop configuration (~/.config/claude/config.json):

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "a11y": {
      "command": "agent-a11y",
      "args": ["mcp", "serve"]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: audit_page
docs.text:
Audit a single page for accessibility issues.

Input:

{
  "url": "https://example.com",
  "level": "AA",
  "version": "2.2"
}

Output:

{
  "score": 85,
  "conformant": false,
  "summary": "WCAG 2.2 Level AA: Non-Conformant (Score: 85/100)",
  "findings": [...]
}

docs.title: audit_site
docs.text:
Audit an entire website by crawling.

Input:

{
  "url": "https://example.com",
  "level": "AA",
  "depth": 2,
  "maxPages": 50
}

docs.title: get_finding_details
docs.text:
Get detailed information about a specific finding.

Input:

{
  "findingId": "finding-123"
}

docs.title: generate_report
docs.text:
Generate a formatted report from audit results.

Input:

{
  "auditId": "audit-456",
  "format": "html"
}

docs.title: get_remediation
docs.text:
Get remediation suggestions for findings.

Input:

{
  "findingId": "finding-123"
}

docs.title: Example Conversation
docs.text:
User: Check the accessibility of example.com

Assistant: I'll audit example.com for accessibility issues.

[Calls audit_page tool]

The audit found 5 accessibility issues:

Score: 85/100 (Non-Conformant)

Critical Issues: - None

Serious Issues: 1. Color contrast insufficient on navigation links 2. Form inputs missing labels

Recommendations: - Increase contrast ratio on nav links to at least 4.5:1 - Add <label> elements for all form inputs

Would you like me to generate a detailed HTML report?

docs.title: Resources
docs.text: The MCP server exposes these resources:

docs.title: wcag_criteria
docs.text:
Access WCAG success criteria information.

wcag://criteria/1.4.3

docs.title: audit_results
docs.text:
Access previous audit results.

audit://results/{audit-id}

docs.title: v0.1.0
docs.text: Release Date: 2026-02-28

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Go Accessibility Toolkit - Comprehensive WCAG compliance testing for Go applications
  • axe-core + LLM - Industry-standard rules enhanced with AI evaluation to reduce false positives
  • Multiple Audit Modes - Single page, site crawling, and user journey testing
  • Rich Report Formats - JSON, HTML, Markdown, VPAT 2.4, WCAG-EM, CSV

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: agent-a11y is a Go accessibility auditing toolkit for WCAG 2.0, 2.1, and 2.2 compliance testing. It combines axe-core rules with LLM-as-a-Judge evaluation to provide accurate accessibility assessments with reduced false positives.

docs.title: WCAG Compliance Testing
docs.text:
• Support for WCAG 2.0, 2.1, and 2.2
  • Levels A, AA, and AAA conformance testing
  • Comprehensive success criteria mapping

docs.title: Audit Modes
docs.text: Mode Description Use Case Single Page Audit one URL Quick checks, CI pipelines Site Crawl Crawl and audit multiple pages Full site audits Journey Follow user journeys via YAML Authenticated flows, SPAs

docs.title: axe-core Integration
docs.text:
• Industry-standard accessibility rules
  • Automatic rule updates
  • Detailed issue descriptions and help links

docs.title: LLM-as-a-Judge
docs.text:
Reduce false positives with AI evaluation:

  • Providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI, Ollama
  • Evaluation: Confirms findings and provides reasoning
  • Confidence: Scores for prioritization

docs.title: Specialized Tests
docs.text:
Browser-based tests beyond axe-core:

  • Keyboard navigation and trap detection
  • Focus visibility and order
  • Reflow at 400% zoom
  • Touch target sizing
  • Text spacing override support
  • Hover content accessibility
  • Flash/flicker detection

docs.title: Report Formats
docs.text: Format Description JSON Machine-readable for CI/CD HTML Interactive reports for stakeholders Markdown Documentation and version control VPAT 2.4 Procurement compliance documentation WCAG-EM W3C evaluation methodology format CSV Spreadsheet export

docs.title: Integration Options
docs.text:
• CLI - Command-line tool for scripts and CI/CD
  • HTTP API - REST API at /audit and /health
  • MCP Server - Integration with AI assistants (Claude)
  • Go Library - Embed in Go applications
  • Multi-Agent Spec - Use in multi-agent workflows

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go install github.com/plexusone/agent-a11y/cmd/agent-a11y@latest

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Basic audit
agent-a11y audit https://example.com

# WCAG 2.2 Level AA with HTML report
agent-a11y audit https://example.com --level AA --version 2.2 -o report.html -f html

# With LLM evaluation
agent-a11y audit https://example.com --llm-provider anthropic --llm-model claude-sonnet-4-20250514

docs.title: Go Library
docs.text:
auditor, _ := a11y.New(
    a11y.WithLevel(a11y.LevelAA),
    a11y.WithVersion(a11y.Version22),
)
defer auditor.Close()

result, _ := auditor.AuditPage(ctx, "https://example.com")
fmt.Println(result.Summary())

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• vibium-go v0.2.1 - Browser automation
  • omnillm v0.13.0 - Multi-provider LLM support
  • multi-agent-spec v0.8.0 - Multi-agent workflows
  • MCP SDK v1.4.0 - Model Context Protocol
